defs: The act of discrediting or disbelieving, or the state of being discredited or disbelieved; as, later accounts have brought the story into discredit.
Hence, some degree of dishonor or disesteem; ill repute; reproach; -- applied to persons or things.
quotes: It is the duty of every Christian to be concerned for the reputation or discredit his life may bring on his profession.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Discredited; p. pr. & vb. n. Discrediting.]

defs: To refuse credence to; not to accept as true; to disbelieve; as, the report is discredited.
To deprive of credibility; to destroy confidence or trust in; to cause disbelief in the accuracy or authority of.
quotes: An occasion might be given to the . . . papists of discrediting our common English Bible.

defs: To deprive of credit or good repute; to bring reproach upon; to make less reputable; to disgrace.
quotes: He. . . least discredits his travels who returns the same man he went.
